rulings.Case Title: CHARLES MCPEEK VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Ford Motor Companys Notice Of Motion To Compel A Further Response To Requests For Admission Nos. 55, 56, 58 59 And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, May 22, 2014 in Department 503, Line 5. Defendant Ford Motor Company's motion to compel a further response to requests for admission nos. 55, 56, 58 & 59 and request for sanctions: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:30a.m., not 9:00a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CONNERS, LAURA, AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR, AND AS et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Defendant Sun Ship, Llc, To Produce Its Custodian Of Records And Person Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And To Produce Documents At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, May 22, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Plaintiffs' motion to compel defendant Sun Ship, LLC to produce its custodian of records and person most knowledgeable for deposition and to produce documents at deposition is off calendar at the moving party's request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JODIE D. COLLINS SR. et al VS. CRANE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Ford Motor Companys Notice Of Motion To Compel A Further Response To Requests For Admission Nos. 47-50 And 52-53 And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, May 22, 2014 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Ford Motor Company's motion to compel a further response to requests for admission nos. 47-50 and 52-53 and request for sanctions is continued on the court's own motion to May 29, 2014. Plaintiffs must deliver a courtesy copy of their opposition to Department 503 by May 22, 2014 at 4:00p.m. (Local Rule 2.6(b).) = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD KOEPKE et al VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order To Require Defendants To Reduce Their Expert Witness Lists And To Consolidate Defense Experts; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Ryan Harris; [Proposed Order]
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, May 22, 2014 in Department 503, Line 4. Plaintiffs' motion for protective order to require defendants to reduce their expert witness lists and to consolidate defense experts is denied. Plaintiffs failed to demonstrate good cause for the Court to grant plaintiffs' requested relief. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m., not 9:00a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
